content: Just as the human cognitive/linguistic concept of reflexion requires active deliberate review of past experience to improve future learning, current LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the structured reflective practice frameworks taught in high school literacy education to reduce inconsistency in LLM self-correction. Reading and writing reflection curricula, which teach learners to evaluate their own output from multiple inconsistent perspectives, align naturally with the core direction of cutting-edge LLM self-reflection research that leverages conflicting solving perspectives.
